scope: Admin auth + repo management dashboard. Magic-link login, allowlist-gated /admin page with is_featured / is_hidden toggles and manual GitHub refresh. Plus Stage 3 polish bundle (URL scheme guard, max-w-container DRY, hero -ml comment).
auth_model: Public user submits email to /admin/login (Server Action requestMagicLink). The action validates email shape, rate-limits (5 per 15min per normalized email), and ALWAYS redirects to /admin/login?sent=1&email=<b64> — when the address is not allowlisted it sleeps 300ms and skips Supabase entirely (timing equalizer); when it IS allowlisted it calls supabase.auth.signInWithOtp with emailRedirectTo derived from request headers. The magic-link hits /auth/callback which calls exchangeCodeForSession, then isAllowlisted() on the session email — if the email isn't in SUPABASE_ADMIN_ALLOWLIST the session is signOut'd and the user is bounced to /admin/login?error=denied. On success, middleware (proxy.ts) enforces the /admin* gate (unauth → login; auth on /admin/login → /admin), and sets X-Robots-Tag: noindex on every /admin* response. The admin page (/admin) re-reads the session server-side and re-verifies the allowlist as defense in depth. All three mutations (toggleFeatured, toggleHidden, refreshRepos) run assertAdmin() first, throwing generic 'not_authorized' on any mismatch. Reads + writes on /admin use the service-role admin client (bypasses RLS so is_hidden rows are visible); the service key is NEVER imported from client modules.
polish_applied: Added src/lib/safe-url.ts with safeHref/safeRepoHref — parses URLs and returns only http(s); falsy otherwise.
IndexRow now renders with safeRepoHref; when unsafe, href='#' + aria-disabled + tabIndex=-1 so the row is still readable but not clickable.
AdminRepoRow uses the same helper from the start — the repo-name anchor and the GitHub-arrow anchor both fall through when the URL is unsafe.

Registered --container-container: 1100px in the @theme block so Tailwind v4 exposes max-w-container. Replaced literal max-w-[1100px] in Hero, SiteHeader, SiteFooter, ProjectIndex, AdminHeader, AdminPage, AdminLogin — one source of truth now.

Hero's xl:-ml-1.5 is KEPT with a clarifying comment. It compensates for Fraunces display's visible left sidebearing on capital 'D' at --text-display-xl (6rem+). Removing it reintroduces the optical misalignment with the eyebrow/paragraph below. Comment explains the intent inline so future reviewers don't strip it as dead code.
supabase_dashboard_config_required: Authentication → URL Configuration → Site URL: set to the production origin (e.g., https://mateushr.com). Local dev works off http://localhost:3000 via the derived origin header.

Authentication → URL Configuration → Redirect URLs: add BOTH https://<prod-host>/auth/callback and http://localhost:3000/auth/callback. Supabase rejects exchangeCodeForSession when the redirect isn't on the allow list.

Authentication → Providers → Email: enable 'Email' provider and ensure 'Confirm email' is ON (magic-link flow uses OTP tokens). 'Enable sign-ups' can stay ON — the allowlist is the real gate, Supabase will happily accept new users and we just signOut the non-allowlisted ones on callback.
(Optional) Authentication → Email Templates → Magic Link: tighten the subject/body copy to match the editorial voice ('mateushr · link de acesso'). Current default works.
vercel_env_required: SUPABASE_ADMIN_ALLOWLIST=mateushr23@gmail.com  (Production, Preview, Development — comma-separated if more than one address is ever added; case-insensitive)
open_questions: Rate-limit store is in-memory per lambda. Acceptable for a single-admin allowlist but means a determined attacker can round-robin lambdas to exceed 5 per 15min. Upgrade path: Vercel KV / Upstash if the allowlist ever grows. Noted but NOT blocking.

CSRF protection for Server Actions relies on Next.js's built-in origin check. next.config.ts does not currently set experimental.serverActions.allowedOrigins — default behavior is strict same-origin in Next 15/16, which is fine for a single-domain deployment. If a custom domain + preview URL pattern ever causes 'Invalid Server Action' errors, add allowedOrigins. Not adding preemptively.
sitemap.xml does not yet exist in the project (none found under src/app/). Once one is added (future SEO pass), /admin* must be excluded. Noted for seo-analyzer stage.
notes_for_orchestrator: Run smoke tests in this order: `npm run build` (Next build + RSC/client boundary validation), `npx tsc --noEmit` (strict-mode pass), and `npx eslint src/` (zero-warning policy from lint-staged).
No new npm packages were installed — server-only is resolved by Next.js via its bundled compiled/server-only module, confirmed present in node_modules/next/dist/compiled/server-only.
The middleware gate and the per-page guard both enforce auth; the allowlist check is ONLY at the page and the server actions (middleware does not import server-only modules).
